user_query:
I need a clean, energy-optimized daily plan for the date in input/date.txt. Please read all of the following and then produce a single markdown plan:
- input/energy.yaml (day start/end, morning priming duration, and energy windows: peak, secondary, recovery, wind_down)
- input/commitments.csv (fixed meetings/appointments with start and end times)
- input/tasks.json (pending tasks with id, title, difficulty, est_minutes, due_date, must_do)

Requirements:
1) Use the provided date and generate a time-blocked schedule from day_start to day_end in input/energy.yaml.
2) Protect the first hour after day_start as "Morning Priming" (no work tasks). Name this block exactly "Morning Priming".
3) Identify the Top 3 priorities based on the combination of must_do=true and the earliest due_date (break ties by earliest due_date). List them under "Top 3 Priorities" by their task titles.
4) Place Top 3 tasks into energy-appropriate windows: deep tasks in the peak window, focused tasks in the secondary window, lighter tasks in wind_down. If a fixed commitment overlaps, split or move the task within the same energy window.
5) Honor all fixed commitments from input/commitments.csv as-is in the schedule (exact start/end times and include the meeting title in that block name).
6) Include a lunch break labeled "Lunch" of at least 30 minutes within the recovery window.
7) Include at least one "Buffer" block of at least 15 minutes.
8) Use 24-hour time format for all time ranges and format each block header exactly as: "### HH:MM - HH:MM: <Block Name>".
9) Under each block, include a "Focus:" line and a checklist of concrete tasks for that block (use the task titles from input/tasks.json for any tasks you place inside blocks), and a "Target:" line.
10) Include the following sections in the markdown (in this order): heading line "# Daily Plan - [Day], [Month] [Date], [Year]" for the given date; "Today's Mission"; "Top 3 Priorities"; "Time-Blocked Schedule"; "Success Criteria" split into Must-Have/Should-Have/Nice-to-Have (Must-Have must include checkboxes for the Top 3 by title); and "Evening Check-In" with three items: "Priority 1 done? YES / NO" etc.

Write the final plan to output/daily_plan.md and make sure the top 3 include the must-do items from the input and that deep/focused tasks are scheduled in the correct windows.
